rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANACE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Compel Lexington Insurance Company Further Reasponses To California Pizza'S Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 7, 2010, line 3. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PIZZA LLC's Motion To Compel Lexington Insurance Company's Further Responses To California Pizza's Request For Production Of Documents is granted. While ordinarily defendants have the option of filing cross-complaints against other culpable parties, equivalent relief is available by way of affirmative defenses. Furthermore, "Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action." C.C.P. Section 2017.010. The disputed request for productions are relevant to California Pizza's 4th affirmative defense of fault of other parties and are therefore discoverable. Lexington must provide further responses and produce all non-privileged documents responsive to the disputed request for productions. To the extent that Lexington claims the items sought are privileged, Lexington is ordered to produce a privilege log. = (302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA VS. SUMMERLIN LIFE AND HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY A et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 7, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT SUMMERLIN LIFE AND HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Equitable tolling does not apply in this case. The plaintiff did not pursue another remedy which would satisfy the first factor set forth in Tarkington v. Cal Unemployment Ins. Appeal Bd. (2009) 172 Cal. App. 4th 1494, 1503. Merely engaging in informal negotiations does not equitably toll the statute. See 65 Butterfield v. Chicago Title Ins. Co. (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 1047, 1063. Plaintiff's delay in filing this action after learning on August 28, 2008 that defendant paid the patient/member was not reasonable. Plaintiff had sufficient opportunity to commence a timely action after learning of the true facts. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS LLP VS. ANDREW EHRENFELD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 7, 2010, line 11. CROSS DEFENDANT WAUKEEN MCCOY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. No reasonable probability that defect can cured by amendment. The court takes judicial notice of the agreement between cross-complainant and McCoy pursuant to E.C. section 452(h). Whether McCoy owed a duty to advise cross-complainant of and pursue a malpractice claim against Goldstein is a question of law, not fact. Nichols v. Keller (1993) 15 Cal. App. 4th 1672, 1682. The court finds that under the retainer agreement, McCoy did not owe a duty to file a malpractice claim or advise of the statute of limitations on the claim. McCoy was hired to help cross-complanaint successfully resolve the underlying suit. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD T FIORY VS. DONN M GURULE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 7, 2010, line 16. DEFENDANT DONN GURULE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is denied. Defendant's belief that the litigation was over was not reasonable. Defendant received a copy of the order allowing amendment and therefore knew the litigation was going forward. The delay in seeking relief was not reasonable, and no explanation is offered for the delay. =(302/CWW)
